title: The Ptah MCP Server — Code Execution as the Interface

captions: Every tool call is a round trip
One tool at a time — ask, wait, repeat
slides.vo: Every MCP tool call is a round trip. The agent asks, waits, reads, and asks again — one tool at a time. And every tool schema rides along in the context window, burning tokens before any real work begins.
Ptah collapses the entire toolbox into a single tool. Instead of calling tools one by one, your agent writes code.

That code runs inside Ptah's sandboxed execution engine, against a live API with twenty-one namespaces. Workspace analysis, AST parsing, semantic search, browser automation, persistent memory, even spawning other agents — chained in a single pass, at machine speed. Intermediate results never touch the context window.

captions: One call in, one result out
slides.vo: One call goes in. One result comes out. The context stays lean, and the agent stays sharp. The Ptah MCP server — code execution as the interface.
